channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tamil Nadu CM Vijay Blames Police for Karur Stampede Lapses, Announces Memorial Plans
item.description: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M.K. Stalin criticized the police for failing to alert him about the surging crowd at his rally in Karur, which led to a stampede on September 27 that claimed 41 lives. Durin...

item.title: Essential Pieces for a Summer Capsule Wardrobe
item.description: This article explores the concept of a summer capsule wardrobe, emphasizing the importance of curating a small, versatile collection of clothing that meets all seasonal needs. It highlights how a caps...

item.title: Celebrating Yolngu Culture Through Ramingining's First Fashion Week
item.description: A remote arts centre in Ramingining, East Arnhem Land, hosted its inaugural fashion week, spotlighting local Aboriginal artists, models, and community members. The event served as a platform to celebr...

item.title: High-Profile Cases Handled by Ex-CCT Chairman Danladi Umar
item.description: The article discusses the legal cases involving former Code of Conduct Tribunal (CCT) Chairman Danladi Umar, who was remanded in custody after being charged with corruption and abuse of office. Umar, ...

item.title: FBI Director Kash Patel Condemns Criticism of His 'Lavish Lifestyle'
item.description: FBI Director Kash Patel responded vehemently to a critical op-ed by MS NOW’s Hayes Brown, who accused him of indulging in a 'lavish lifestyle' funded by taxpayers. The critique highlighted Patel's all...

item.title: Sindh Government Orders Karachi Private School to Reverse Unlawful Fee Increases
item.description: The Sindh government has directed a private school in Karachi to immediately reverse unlawful fee hikes imposed during summer holidays. In a formal letter dated July 10, the Directorate of Inspection ...

item.title: North West Police Make Progress in Investigating Activist's Murder
item.description: The police commissioner of the North West province, Maj-Gen Ryno Naidoo, is set to brief the media on Friday afternoon regarding the arrest of two suspects in the murder of activist Thato Molosankwe. ...

item.title: Fish Trade Dispute Sparks Violence and Tragic Death
item.description: A violent clash over fish trade has resulted in a fatal killing, highlighting the dangers of unresolved commercial disputes in local markets. The incident occurred when traders involved in the fish bu...

item.title: African Teams' World Cup Struggles Linked to Tactical Fatigue, Says Coach Mogudi
item.description: University of Johannesburg (UJ) football coach Karabo Mogudi has highlighted tactical fatigue as a critical factor behind African teams' struggles during the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Despite a strong grou...

item.title: Pfas contamination in Lancashire linked to elevated kidney cancer rates and community health concerns
item.description: Residents of Thornton-Cleveleys and surrounding areas are grappling with the long-term effects of Pfas contamination from a former chemical plant. A government study revealed higher-than-expected kidn...

item.title: Ontario Man Gets 20-Year Sentence in U.S. for Leading Cross-Border Drug Ring
item.description: Guramrit Sidhu of Brampton, Ontario, has been sentenced to 20 years in a U.S. federal prison for orchestrating a cross-border drug trafficking operation that spanned from September 2020 to February 20...

item.title: Dar es Salaam Trade Fair Marks 50 Years of Economic Growth in Tanzania
item.description: The Dar es Salaam International Trade Fair, known as Sabasaba, celebrates its 50th anniversary as a pivotal platform for Tanzanian businesses. Over five decades, the exhibition has generated over 129 ...

item.title: NCPC approves Trump's controversial arch design despite public objections
item.description: President Donald Trump's Monumental Arch project near Washington, D.C., received initial approval from the National Capital Planning Commission (NCPC), which is heavily stacked with his appointees. De...

item.title: U.S.-Iran Ceasefire Halted as Diplomatic Efforts Resume Amid Hormuz Tensions
item.description: The article details the current state of the U.S.-Iran conflict, highlighting a temporary pause in hostilities as mediators work to restart diplomatic talks. U.S. officials claim deliberate strikes ar...

item.title: Exploring Hypocrisy and Poverty Through Poetry: A Reflection on Language Extinction
item.description: The article features an excerpt from the poetry collection 'Served Cold from the Refrigerator' by Sutputra Radheye, delving into themes of societal hypocrisy, poverty, and the extinction of languages....

item.title: Philippines ranks among Vietnam's top tourist sources
item.description: The article highlights that the Philippines is one of Vietnam's largest sources of tourists, underscoring the growing bilateral tourism relationship between the two nations. Published by ABS-CBN News ...

item.title: NRL Round 19: Wests Tigers vs Warriors Match Recap
item.description: The Sydney Morning Herald's NRL Round 19 coverage highlights the intense clash between the Wests Tigers and Warriors at Campbelltown. The match, which took place on July 10, 2026, featured dramatic mo...

item.title: Melissa O'Neil's Holistic Approach to Health and Wellness
item.description: Melissa O'Neil's weight loss journey emphasizes long-term health over quick fixes. She combines intense training with a positive mindset towards nutrition, focusing on fitness, mindful eating, and men...

item.title: Centre tightens regulations on high-alcohol medicinal products to curb misuse
item.description: The Indian government has amended drug regulations to bring high-alcohol medicinal formulations under stricter control. Previously exempted under Schedule K, these products now fall under Schedule H1,...

item.title: Man Arrested After Home Invasion Incident Involving Nursing Students
item.description: A 34-year-old man has been taken into police custody following a home invasion incident in Perth's west. The alleged offender, dressed only in a cap, allegedly breached the residence of three young nu...
